feedback.details: Diagram validates successfully with mmdc (exit code 0). Uses proper C4 container syntax with escaped HTML entities (`#lt;br#gt;`) for line breaks. No syntax errors or rendering issues detected.

All 10+ required containers present: API Gateway (line 9), Orchestrator (line 17), Model Router (line 18), Local Model Instances Qwen/Nemotron (lines 20-24), Frontier API Adapter (line 25), Monitoring (line 27), Health Check Service (line 26), Administrative Interface (line 30), Persistent Storage (Consul lines 31,37 and Vault lines 32,38), and External System boundary markers (lines 36-43). All labeled with technology stack, responsibility, and replication factors (e.g., '2x').

Complete data flow: API Gateway → Orchestrator → Model Router → Local/Frontier Models. Monitoring exposed (line 68), health checks bidirectional (line 73 BiRel), self-learning async traces (lines 54-56). Synchronous flows use solid lines, asynchronous use 'dashed' (lines 54-60, 74). All flows correctly labeled.

Fully aligned: Nomad topology (lines 36, 70-73), Consul/Vault integration (lines 31-32, 66-67), Golang labels throughout, bifrost usage (lines 45, 18-21), hardware telemetry (lines 17, 20-24 with GPU/CPU metrics), VRAM-aware routing (line 46), network segmentation with DMZ (line 8), Firewall (line 11), and Internal VPC (line 12).

Narrative contains exactly 200 words (within 150-250 threshold), explicitly stating word count. Covers all required aspects: container purposes, key interactions, deployment topology, developer journey (zero-code-change integration), and operations journey (administrative interface).

Horizontal scaling demonstrated: Qwen/Nemotron load balancers (lines 19, 22) connect to pools of adapters (lines 20-21, 23-24). Configuration artifacts separated from code in dedicated Nomad Configuration boundary (lines 13-16). Adding new LLM shown as Nomad job spec update via admin_interface (line 70) without code changes.

Complete failure handling: Fallback paths between Qwen/Nemotron (lines 76-77), circuit breaker patterns (lines 47-49, 76-77), <500ms timeouts (lines 45, 47-49, 76-77), dead letter queue with 3-retry threshold (line 74), and automatic rerouting via Model Router circuit breaker logic.

Security zones explicit: DMZ for API Gateway (line 8), Firewall boundary (line 11), Internal VPC (line 12). mTLS shown on internal flows (lines 45-73). Cost differentiation: 'Low Cost' (lines 47-48, 76-77) vs 'High Cost' (line 49). VRAM tiers: Qwen 24GB (line 39), Nemotron 48GB (line 40) affecting routing decisions.

overall_summary: This is an exceptional C4 container diagram that fully satisfies all sprint contract criteria. The diagram validates with no Mermaid syntax errors, contains all required containers with proper C4 Level 2 detail (technology, replication, responsibilities), and accurately represents the llm-switch architecture from the PRD. Key strengths include: complete failure handling visibility with circuit breakers, fallback paths, and dead letter queues; explicit security boundaries (DMZ, Firewall, Internal VPC) with mTLS flows; clear distinction between synchronous (solid) and asynchronous (dashed) relationships; proper horizontal scaling notation with load balancers and adapter pools; and configuration/artifact separation from application code. The 200-word narrative falls within the required 150-250 range and explicitly states its word count. No gaps or issues found across any of the 8 evaluation criteria.
